div.p: Terms provide logical time ordering across the cluster Each term has AT MOST ONE leader (quorum intersection guarantee)
Terms advance monotonically; nodes update on higher-term RPCs

p.strong: When receiving RPC with term T:

ol.li:
If T > currentTerm:
Set currentTerm = T
Set votedFor = "" (reset vote)
Transition to Follower
PERSIST to disk before responding

div.p: Key Invariant: currentTerm is MONOTONIC Nodes never decrease their term. Terms only increase through:

ul.li: Receiving higher-term RPC (from leader/candidate) This ensures: At most one leader per term
Election 1: {A, B, C} vote for leader X
Election 2: {C, D, E} vote for leader Y Node C is in BOTH quorums → C cannot vote twice in same term Therefore: Impossible to have 2 leaders in one term
